Active Guardrails
- [required] Operator-surface work is not done until the required surface gates pass: `ai-workflow dogfood` when applicable, and `workflow-audit` whenever workflow docs or operator contracts changed. (Project Guidelines)
- [strong] Treat shell mode as a planning and orchestration surface, not as permission to skip workflow discipline. (Manual)
- [required] The workflow DB is the canonical operational state. `kanban.md` and `epics.md` are controlled projections, and direct edits must be reconciled instead of silently overwritten. (Project Guidelines)
- [required] If the behavior claims workflow awareness, it must be grounded in canonical DB-backed state instead of prompt glue, transient locals, or duplicated surface-specific memory. (Project Guidelines)
- [required] Workflow state must stay honest before closure: sync, projections, ticket lane, and archived state should all agree with the implemented result. (Project Guidelines)
- [required] Failure honesty is part of done: if the system lacks evidence, capability, or readiness, it must surface that clearly instead of bluffing. (Project Guidelines)
- [strong] Use `ai-workflow` first for project status, ticket lookup, projections, and guideline extraction; fall back to raw shell search/read only when the workflow tool cannot answer. (AGENTS)
- [required] Treat the workflow DB as canonical state, and treat projections as readable outputs that must stay reconciled. (Manual)

Suggested Validation
- Level: small-ticket
- Recommendation: Run quick but meaningful unit or module tests for the touched behavior.
- C
... [truncated]
